title: NVIDIA GB200 NVL72: Liquid Cooling for Peak Performance | Crusoe posted on the topic | LinkedIn
text:
The unprecedented compute density of the NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 requires liquid cooling for peak performance, energy efficiency, reliability, and total cost of ownership (TCO) savings. Crusoe built our first liquid-cooled deployment in Iceland to accelerate your most ambitious workloads. Powered by 100% renewable geothermal and hydro energy, the synergy between our advanced, closed-loop liquid cooling systems and the naturally cool climate allows us to maximize efficiency. We are experts in deploying and managing advanced cooling technologies at scale. Our NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters in Iceland are a testament to this, running today in one of the world's most innovative energy environments. 🌋 Learn more 🔗: https://lnkd.in/gSenkYuA
Actually, all of these racks are cooled with both liquid and air cooling. Nvidia is very clear that liquid is reserved for high density heat loads and are not cost effective for lower powered equipment.
Incredible innovation from Crusoe! Leveraging liquid cooling for the NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 in Iceland’s renewable energy hub is a game-changer for performance and sustainability. 🌋 The synergy of advanced cooling and a naturally cool climate sets a new standard for efficiency in high-performance computing. Excited to see how this powers ambitious workloads! #SustainableTech #HPC #Innovation #RenewableEnergy
Are you referring to Alberta in Canada? There are currently good conditions for temperature, gas, and hydro energy, and electricity bills are lower. GO CANADA! Transalta will have soon many new DC customers 🦾💫💫💫
"Impressive innovation in data center cooling! The combination of liquid cooling and renewable energy in Iceland demonstrates a perfect synergy for peak performance and sustainability. It's exciting to see how advanced technologies like NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 are pushing the boundaries of energy efficiency and scalability. A great leap towards a more sustainable future in computing!"
Recycle the excess heat. There is a new age of thinking on industrial heat, maybe the heat pipes can be utilized from two angles! Find some industrial heat experts and tell them you have free excess heat that can be recycled.
There is no best, only better.🫡💪
